summary: Backend + Frontend e2e testing for Option B auth hardening, webhooks routing, and refactor. 22/22 backend pytest tests pass; Playwright covers signup->dashboard->onramp quote->logout for USER, and signup->dev-portal->create/reveal/revoke key for DEVELOPER. All required data-testids present.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth wiring is clean: access(15m)/refresh(7d) JWTs with explicit 'type' claim and decode helpers that reject the wrong type — verified end-to-end (refresh with access token => 401, refresh with refresh token => new pair).

Token rotation works but JWT timestamps (iat/exp) are second-resolution; if a client refreshes within the same second as issuance the resulting JWT will be byte-identical. Not a security issue, but worth noting if you ever add a server-side rotation counter / refresh-token blacklist.
Security headers middleware applies to every response (verified on /api/health and /api/ramp/prices).
Stripe webhook route mounted correctly: 400 on missing Stripe-Signature, 200 with status=error on bogus signature (graceful fallback because STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is intentionally unset).
Frontend axios silent-refresh interceptor uses an in-flight singleton (refreshInFlight) — good, prevents thundering-herd refresh. Falls back to clearTokens() + reject on failure.

Minor UX observation (not a bug): after DEVELOPER role signup the user lands on /dashboard rather than /dev-portal; the nav link 'Dev Portal' is visible so flow works, but landing the new developer directly on /dev-portal would be a nicer first-run experience.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (new - 22 tests across auth, refresh, /me, security headers, Stripe webhook, ramp regression, HMAC, dev portal)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (signup, dashboard mount, onramp quote, logout, dev signup, dev-portal mount, create-key, reveal secret, revoke key)

test_credentials: Tests create unique TEST_<uuid>@example.com / TestPass123! users on every run via /api/auth/register. No persistent seeded users needed.
seed_data_creation: None — all data is ephemeral test users + transient platform API keys (revoked at end of test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests are at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py and run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Silent-refresh interceptor was verified at the code-review level (single in-flight refresh, persists new pair, retries original request once). End-to-end interceptor behavior in a real browser would require artificially expiring the access token (e.g., localStorage override) — out of scope for this run. Tokens use second-precision iat/exp, so any rotation-difference assertion needs a >=1s gap before refresh.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. One transient assertion (token rotation produces different JWT string) initially tripped on identical iat/exp seconds; fixed in the test by introducing a 1.2s sleep before /auth/refresh. Production behavior is correct — new tokens are issued every call, the JWT just serializes to the same string when the entire payload (sub/email/role/type/iat/exp) is identical.
